mitre_attack: T1592.004 - Gather Victim Network Information: Client Configurations
cwe: CWE-209 - Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

threat.title: API Version Disclosure Enabling Downgrade Attacks
threat.description: When organizations maintain multiple API versions without proper sunset policies, attackers can enumerate and downgrade to older API versions containing unpatched security vulnerabilities. Legacy API endpoints often lack modern security controls like rate limiting, proper authentication, input validation, and authorization checks. This creates a vulnerability surface where attackers exploit deprecated versions to bypass security improvements implemented in newer versions.

versions_affected: Any API with multiple active versions

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker discovers API documentation or uses tools like Burp Suite/Postman to enumerate available API versions
2. Identifies multiple active versions through URL patterns (/api/v1/, /api/v2/, /api/v3/)
3. Tests authentication endpoint across versions: POST /api/v1/auth/login vs /api/v3/auth/login
4. Discovers v1 API lacks rate limiting and accepts weak passwords (no complexity enforcement)
5. Performs credential stuffing attack against v1 endpoint with 10,000 requests/minute
6. Successfully authenticates as admin user and accesses v1 endpoints that lack proper authorization checks
7. Escalates to v3 API using stolen session token that is valid across all versions
8. Exfiltrates sensitive customer data through v1 endpoint that returns full records without field filtering
prerequisites: Multiple API versions actively deployed and accessible
Security improvements in newer versions not backported to legacy versions
Shared authentication tokens or sessions across API versions
No API version usage monitoring or alerting

cve_references.description: Rack middleware vulnerability allowing header injection in older versions while newer versions were patched
Atlassian Crowd and Crowd Data Center authentication bypass in REST API v1
Laravel Framework API authentication bypass in legacy versions allowing privilege escalation

real_world_breaches.impact: Exposed private user data of millions including location, age, weight through legacy API v1
real_world_breaches.root_cause: API v1 lacked proper authorization checks while v2 had implemented secure access controls. Researchers discovered v1 endpoints were still active and accessible.

real_world_breaches.impact: Data of 54 million customers compromised through exploitation of legacy API endpoints
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attacker accessed deprecated API version that lacked rate limiting and proper authentication, allowing unauthorized database queries

real_world_breaches.impact: Public API exposed transaction data of 200+ million users without proper privacy controls
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Legacy API v1 defaulted transactions to public visibility without user consent, while v2 API implemented proper privacy settings

real_world_breaches.impact: 60 million user accounts exposed through API vulnerability allowing unauthorized access
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Legacy API version allowed any authenticated user to query arbitrary user account data without authorization checks

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Legacy API v1 authentication bypass allowing access to all user accounts on major fintech platform
API version downgrade attack exposing PII through deprecated endpoint lacking field filtering
Rate limiting bypass via API v1 allowing credential stuffing attack against authentication endpoint

security_research.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 - API9: Improper Assets Management
security_research.key_finding: Outdated API versions are often more vulnerable due to lack of patching and security updates. Organizations frequently lose track of deprecated versions still accessible to attackers.

security_research.title: The Hidden Costs of API Versioning
security_research.key_finding: 73% of organizations surveyed had at least 3 API versions in production, with average of 5.2 security vulnerabilities present in deprecated versions

security_research.title: REST API Versioning Strategies and Security Implications
security_research.key_finding: URI versioning (most common approach) creates easily discoverable attack surface. Study found 68% of legacy API endpoints lacked security controls present in current versions.

mitigations.title: Implement API Version Sunset Policy with Express.js Middleware
mitigations.description: Enforce automatic deprecation and sunset of old API versions using middleware that tracks version usage, warns clients about deprecation, and blocks access after sunset date. This ensures legacy versions cannot be exploited indefinitely.
nist_csf: ID.AM-2 - Software platforms and applications are inventoried
PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
DE.CM-7 - Monitoring for unauthorized activity is performed
validation_steps: Verify deprecated version returns HTTP 410 after sunset date
Confirm Deprecation and Sunset headers are present in responses
Test that current version (v3) has no deprecation warnings
Validate monitoring logs capture deprecated API usage
Check that clients receive migration guide URLs

mitigations.title: AWS API Gateway API Version Lifecycle Management
mitigations.description: Use AWS API Gateway deployment stages and API keys to control API version access, enforce rate limiting per version, and automatically sunset deprecated versions using Lambda authorizers.

code_example.code:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: API Gateway with Version Lifecycle Management

Parameters:
  V1SunsetDate:
    Type: String
    Default: '2025-06-01T00:00:00Z'
    Description: Sunset date for API v1

Resources:
  # REST API with multiple versions
  MyAPI:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      Name: VersionedAPI
      StageName: prod
      TracingEnabled: true
      MethodSettings:
        - ResourcePath: '/*'
          HttpMethod: '*'
          LoggingLevel: INFO
          DataTraceEnabled: true
          MetricsEnabled: true
      DefinitionBody:
        openapi: 3.0.0
        info:
          title: Versioned API
          version: '3.0'
        paths:
          /v1/{proxy+}:
            x-amazon-apigateway-any-method:
              x-amazon-apigateway-integration:
                type: aws_proxy
                uri: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${V1Function.Arn}/invocations'
                httpMethod: POST
              parameters:
                - name: proxy
                  in: path
                  required: true
                  schema:
                    type: string
              responses:
                '410':
                  description: API version sunset
                  headers:
                    Sunset:
                      schema:
                        type: string
          /v3/{proxy+}:
            x-amazon-apigateway-any-method:
              x-amazon-apigateway-integration:
                type: aws_proxy
                uri: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${V3Function.Arn}/invocations'
                httpMethod: POST

  # Lambda authorizer to enforce version sunset
  VersionAuthorizerFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.handler
      Runtime: nodejs18.x
      Environment:
        Variables:
          V1_SUNSET_DATE: !Ref V1SunsetDate
      InlineCode: |
        exports.handler = async (event) => {
          const path = event.path;
          const versionMatch = path.match(/^\/v(\d+)/);
          
          if (!versionMatch) {
            return {
              statusCode: 400,
              body: JSON.stringify({ error: 'API version not specified' })
            };
          }
          
          const version = parseInt(versionMatch[1]);
          const v1SunsetDate = new Date(process.env.V1_SUNSET_DATE);
          const now = new Date();
          
          // Block sunset versions
          if (version === 1 && now > v1SunsetDate) {
            return {
              statusCode: 410,
              headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'Sunset': v1SunsetDate.toISOString(),
                'Link': '<https://api.example.com/v3>; rel="successor-version"'
              },
              body: JSON.stringify({
                error: 'API version sunset',
                message: `API v1 was sunset on ${v1SunsetDate.toISOString()}`,
                replacementVersion: 'v3'
              })
            };
          }
          
          // Add deprecation warnings
          if (version === 1) {
            const daysUntilSunset = Math.ceil((v1SunsetDate - now) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
            console.warn({
              event: 'deprecated_api_usage',
              version: `v${version}`,
              path: event.path,
              sourceIp: event.requestContext.identity.sourceIp,
              daysUntilSunset
            });
          }
          
          return {
            principalId: 'api-version-check',
            policyDocument: {
              Version: '2012-10-17',
              Statement: [{
                Action: 'execute-api:Invoke',
                Effect: 'Allow',
                Resource: event.methodArn
              }]
            },
            context: {
              apiVersion: `v${version}`,
              deprecated: version === 1 ? 'true' : 'false'
            }
          };
        };

  # Usage plan with rate limiting per version
  V1UsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: API-v1-Restricted
      Description: Restricted rate limits for deprecated v1
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref MyAPI
          Stage: prod
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 10
        RateLimit: 5
      Quota:
        Limit: 1000
        Period: DAY

  V3UsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: API-v3-Standard
      Description: Standard rate limits for current v3
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref MyAPI
          Stage: prod
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 100
        RateLimit: 50
      Quota:
        Limit: 100000
        Period: DAY

  # CloudWatch Alarm for deprecated API usage
  DeprecatedAPIAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: DeprecatedAPIUsageHigh
      AlarmDescription: Alert when deprecated API v1 usage exceeds threshold
      MetricName: Count
      Namespace: AWS/ApiGateway
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 100
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: ApiName
          Value: VersionedAPI
        - Name: Stage
          Value: prod
        - Name: Resource
          Value: /v1/{proxy+}

Outputs:
  ApiEndpoint:
    Description: API Gateway endpoint
    Value: !Sub 'https://${MyAPI}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod'
  
  CurrentVersion:
    Description: Current API version
    Value: v3
  
  V1SunsetDate:
    Description: V1 API sunset date
    Value: !Ref V1SunsetDate

mitigations.title: Spring Boot API Version Security with Sunset Enforcement
mitigations.description: Implement API versioning in Spring Boot using custom annotations and interceptors to enforce version lifecycle policies, add security headers, and monitor deprecated version usage.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
DE.CM-7 - Monitoring for unauthorized activity is performed

Test deprecated version returns proper headers
Verify sunset versions return HTTP 410
Confirm logging captures deprecated API usage
Validate automatic sunset enforcement after date passes

mitigations.title: API Version Discovery Prevention with Azure API Management
mitigations.description: Use Azure API Management policies to hide deprecated API versions from discovery, enforce version-specific security policies, and automatically redirect legacy requests to current versions with proper migration support.

code_example.code:
<!-- API Management Policy - Applied at API level -->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <!-- Extract API version from URL path or header -->
        <set-variable name="apiVersion" value="@{
            var path = context.Request.Url.Path;
            var versionMatch = Regex.Match(path, @"/api/(v\d+)/");
            if (versionMatch.Success) {
                return versionMatch.Groups[1].Value;
            }
            // Check header as fallback
            return context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("API-Version", "v3");
        }" />
        
        <!-- Define version lifecycle dates -->
        <set-variable name="v1SunsetDate" value="2025-06-01T00:00:00Z" />
        <set-variable name="v1DeprecatedDate" value="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" />
        <set-variable name="currentVersion" value="v3" />
        
        <!-- Block sunset versions -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiVersion") == "v1" && DateTime.UtcNow > DateTime.Parse((string)context.Variables["v1SunsetDate"]))">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="410" reason="Gone" />
                    <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-header name="Sunset" exists-action="override">
                        <value>@((string)context.Variables["v1SunsetDate"])</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-header name="Link" exists-action="override">
                        <value><https://api.example.com/v3>; rel="successor-version"</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>@{
                        return new JObject(
                            new JProperty("error", "API version sunset"),
                            new JProperty("message", "API v1 was sunset on " + context.Variables["v1SunsetDate"]),
                            new JProperty("replacementVersion", "v3"),
                            new JProperty("migrationGuide", "https://docs.example.com/api/migration/v1-to-v3")
                        ).ToString();
                    }</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>
        
        <!-- Add deprecation warnings for deprecated versions -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiVersion") == "v1" && DateTime.UtcNow > DateTime.Parse((string)context.Variables["v1DeprecatedDate"]))">
                <set-header name="Deprecation" exists-action="override">
                    <value>version="v1"</value>
                </set-header>
                <set-header name="Sunset" exists-action="override">
                    <value>@((string)context.Variables["v1SunsetDate"])</value>
                </set-header>
                <set-header name="Link" exists-action="override">
                    <value><https://api.example.com/v3>; rel="successor-version"</value>
                </set-header>
                <set-header name="Warning" exists-action="override">
                    <value>@{
                        var sunsetDate = DateTime.Parse((string)context.Variables["v1SunsetDate"]);
                        var daysUntilSunset = (sunsetDate - DateTime.UtcNow).Days;
                        return $"299 - \"API v1 is deprecated and will be sunset in {daysUntilSunset} days\"";
                    }</value>
                </set-header>
                
                <!-- Log deprecated API usage to Application Insights -->
                <log-to-eventhub logger-id="deprecated-api-logger">
                    @{
                        return new JObject(
                            new JProperty("eventType", "deprecated_api_usage"),
                            new JProperty("apiVersion", context.Variables["apiVersion"]),
                            new JProperty("path", context.Request.Url.Path),
                            new JProperty("method", context.Request.Method),
                            new JProperty("clientIp", context.Request.IpAddress),
                            new JProperty("userAgent", context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("User-Agent", "unknown")),
                            new JProperty("timestamp", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o")),
                            new JProperty("daysUntilSunset", (DateTime.Parse((string)context.Variables["v1SunsetDate"]) - DateTime.UtcNow).Days)
                        ).ToString();
                    }
                </log-to-eventhub>
            </when>
        </choose>
        
        <!-- Enforce stricter rate limits on deprecated versions -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiVersion") == "v1")">
                <rate-limit calls="10" renewal-period="60" />
                <quota calls="1000" renewal-period="86400" />
            </when>
            <otherwise>
                <!-- Standard rate limits for current version -->
                <rate-limit calls="100" renewal-period="60" />
                <quota calls="100000" renewal-period="86400" />
            </otherwise>
        </choose>
        
        <!-- Remove version info from error responses to prevent enumeration -->
        <set-header name="X-API-Version" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="Server" exists-action="override">
            <value>API Gateway</value>
        </set-header>
        
        <base />
    </inbound>
    
    <backend>
        <base />
    </backend>
    
    <outbound>
        <!-- Add security headers -->
        <set-header name="X-Content-Type-Options" exists-action="override">
            <value>nosniff</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="X-Frame-Options" exists-action="override">
            <value>DENY</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="Strict-Transport-Security" exists-action="override">
            <value>max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains</value>
        </set-header>
        
        <!-- Remove sensitive headers from responses -->
        <set-header name="X-AspNet-Version" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="X-Powered-By" exists-action="delete" />
        
        <base />
    </outbound>
    
    <on-error>
        <!-- Prevent version disclosure in error messages -->
        <set-header name="X-API-Version" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-body>@{
            return new JObject(
                new JProperty("error", "An error occurred"),
                new JProperty("message", "Please contact support"),
                new JProperty("timestamp", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o"))
            ).ToString();
        }</set-body>
        <base />
    </on-error>
</policies>

nist_csf: PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
DE.CM-7 - Monitoring for unauthorized activity is performed
PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Deploy APIM policy to Azure API Management instance
Test v1 API returns 410 after sunset date
Verify rate limiting is 10x stricter for v1 than v3
Confirm Event Hub receives deprecated API usage logs
Validate version information is removed from error responses

indicators: High volume of requests to deprecated API versions (e.g., /api/v1/*)
Sequential requests testing multiple API versions from same IP
Requests with unusual User-Agent strings testing version patterns
Authentication failures followed by version enumeration attempts
Successful authentication to deprecated endpoints from unexpected geolocations
API requests with manually crafted version headers
Spike in traffic to legacy endpoints after they were announced as deprecated
Different authentication tokens being tested across API versions

log_queries.query:
// Detect API version enumeration attempts
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where CsUriStem matches regex @"/api/v\d+/"
| extend ApiVersion = extract(@"/api/(v\d+)/", 1, CsUriStem)
| summarize 
    UniqueVersions = dcount(ApiVersion),
    RequestCount = count(),
    StatusCodes = make_set(ScStatus)
    by CIp, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where UniqueVersions >= 3  // Testing multiple versions
| where RequestCount > 20
| order by RequestCount desc
| project TimeGenerated, CIp, UniqueVersions, RequestCount, StatusCodes

// Monitor deprecated API version usage
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where CsUriStem contains "/api/v1/"
| extend 
    Endpoint = extract(@"/api/v1/(\w+)", 1, CsUriStem),
    IsSuccess = ScStatus between (200 .. 299)
| summarize 
    TotalRequests = count(),
    SuccessfulRequests = countif(IsSuccess),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(CIp),
    UniqueEndpoints = dcount(Endpoint)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where TotalRequests > 100  // Threshold for investigation
| extend SuccessRate = round(todouble(SuccessfulRequests) / TotalRequests * 100, 2)
| project TimeGenerated, TotalRequests, SuccessRate, UniqueIPs, UniqueEndpoints

index=api_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| rex field=uri_path "/api/(?<api_version>v\d+)/(?<endpoint>\w+)"
| where isnotnull(api_version)
| stats 
    dc(api_version) as unique_versions,
    count as request_count,
    values(api_version) as tested_versions,
    values(status) as status_codes
    by clientip, _time span=5m
| where unique_versions >= 3
| where request_count > 20
| eval threat_score = unique_versions * 10 + request_count / 10
| where threat_score > 50
| sort - threat_score
| table _time, clientip, unique_versions, tested_versions, request_count, status_codes, threat_score

index=api_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| rex field=uri_path "/api/(?<api_version>v\d+)/"
| transaction clientip maxspan=30m
| where mvcount(api_version) > 1
| eval version_progression = mvjoin(api_version, " -> ")
| search version_progression="v3 -> v1" OR version_progression="v2 -> v1"
| stats 
    count as downgrade_attempts,
    values(uri_path) as accessed_endpoints,
    values(status) as response_codes
    by clientip, user_agent
| where downgrade_attempts > 0
| eval severity = if(downgrade_attempts > 5, "high", "medium")
| table clientip, user_agent, downgrade_attempts, accessed_endpoints, response_codes, severity

event.category: "web" AND url.path: /api/v*/
| stats 
    cardinality(url.version) as unique_versions,
    count() as request_count,
    values(http.response.status_code) as status_codes
    by source.ip, time.bucket(5m)
| where unique_versions >= 3 AND request_count > 20
| sort request_count desc

fields @timestamp, @message, request.path, request.version, source.ip
| parse @message /\/api\/(?<version>v\d+)\//
| filter ispresent(version)
| stats count(*) as request_count, 
        count_distinct(version) as unique_versions,
        values(version) as tested_versions
        by source.ip, bin(5m)
| filter unique_versions >= 3 and request_count > 20
| sort request_count desc

log_queries.siem: AWS CloudWatch Insights - Authentication Bypass Detection
log_queries.query:
fields @timestamp, request.path, request.version, auth.success, user.role
| parse @message /\/api\/(?<version>v\d+)\//
| filter version = "v1" and auth.success = true and user.role = "admin"
| stats count(*) as admin_access_count by source.ip, user.id, bin(1h)
| filter admin_access_count > 10
| sort admin_access_count desc
